\title{First-Stage Results}
\subtitle{Sector-Level BNDES Reallocation}
\author{}
\date{February 2026}

\begin{document}

\begin{frame}
\titlepage
\end{frame}

% ===========================================================================
% OVERVIEW
% ===========================================================================

\begin{frame}{Baseline Specification}
\begin{block}{First-stage equation}
$$\Delta s_{mjt} = \alpha_{mj} + \alpha_{jt} + \beta \cdot Z^{\text{mayor}}_{mjt} + \gamma \cdot \text{ExposureControl}_{mj} + \varepsilon_{mjt}$$
\end{block}
\medskip
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{Sector variable}: sector groups ($\sim$10 groups, Manufacturing split 3 ways)
\item \textbf{Baseline weights}: cycle-specific (pre-election year)
\item \textbf{Alignment}: coalition level
\item \textbf{FE}: muni$\times$sector + sector$\times$year
\item \textbf{Clustering}: two-way (municipality + sector group)
\item \textbf{Control}: sum of baseline exposure weights across parties
\end{itemize}
\medskip
{\footnotesize Identification: cross-municipality variation in political exposure within sector-year cells.}
\end{frame}

% ===========================================================================
% SUMMARY
% ===========================================================================

\begin{frame}{Key Takeaways}
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{Baseline} (sector$\times$year FE): coalition instruments are weak ($F < 2$ for Mayor alone)
\item \textbf{Muni$\times$year FE} (Table 2): Mayor significant ($\hat\beta \approx 0.020$, $F \approx 6$)
\item \textbf{Year FE only} (Table 3): strongest first stage ($\hat\beta \approx 0.018$, $F \approx 25$)
\item \textbf{Party alignment} (Table 4): much stronger ($F = 12$ for Mayor, $F = 673$ for M+G)
\item \textbf{No exposure control} (Table 5): coefficients drop and lose significance
\item \textbf{2002-fixed weights} (Table 6): weaker than cycle-specific across the board
\item \textbf{Levels} (Table 7): Mayor significant and negative ($\hat\beta \approx -0.025$, $F \approx 7.6$)
\end{itemize}
\bigskip
{\color{alertred} Main concern: coalition instruments are weak with sector$\times$year FE. Party-level instruments are much stronger. Consider party as primary or investigate why coalition is absorbed by sector$\times$year FE.}
\end{frame}

\end{document}
